headline: Micron (MU) Lands GM Supply Deal And Backs Trump Accounts With $250 Million
summary: Micron Technology (NasdaqGS:MU) and General Motors announced a long term supply agreement for automotive memory and storage platforms for future vehicle production. The partnership focuses on securing semiconductors for next generation vehicles as computing and data requirements in cars continue to rise. Separately, Micron committed US$250 million to the new national Trump Accounts child savings program, currently the largest corporate pledge to the initiative. For investors watching Micron...

headline: General Motors shares rise after securing long-term Micron chip supply
summary: GM strengthens semiconductor supply chain with Micron agreementShares of General Motors (NYSE:GM) edged higher on Wednesday after the automaker unveiled a strategic partnership with Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) aimed at securing a long-term supply of memory and storage components for its vehicles. The Strategic Customer Agreement is designed to ensure GM has dependable access to the memory and storage technologies needed to support large-scale vehicle production, while addressing ongoing supply

headline: Micron, GM sign semiconductor supply agreement for vehicles
summary: Micron Technology and General Motors have signed a long-term supply agreement for ‌memory and storage platforms used in vehicle ‌production, the companies said on Wednesday. Under the agreement, GM will ​secure supplies of memory and storage chips, while the companies work together to develop future technologies. Surging investments towards AI-powered data centers have boosted demand for memory ‌chips, tightening supply ⁠and raising prices across industries, including automakers.

content.title: Micron, GM sign semiconductor supply agreement for vehicles
content.summary: Micron Technology and General Motors have signed a long-term supply agreement for ‌memory and storage platforms used in vehicle ‌production, the companies said on Wednesday. Under the agreement, GM will ​secure supplies of memory and storage chips, while the companies work together to develop future technologies. Surging investments towards AI-powered data centers have boosted demand for memory ‌chips, tightening supply ⁠and raising prices across industries, including automakers.
